faqs.a: A structural insulated panel is a high-performance building panel that combines insulation and structure in one piece — a rigid insulating core (high-density EPS) bonded between two strong facings. Because the insulation is built in and the panel carries load, SIPs let you frame, insulate and line a wall or roof in a single step, giving you a faster, stronger and far more energy-efficient envelope than traditional stick framing.

faqs.q: How quickly can a build be locked up with SIPs?
faqs.a: Much faster than conventional construction. Panels arrive pre-cut and pre-finished and lock together on site, so many projects reach lock-up in as little as 3–4 days. Less time on site means lower labour costs, fewer weather delays and a quicker path to a weather-tight, insulated shell.

faqs.q: How well do the panels insulate, and can they meet energy ratings?
faqs.a: Very well. The continuous EPS core dramatically reduces thermal bridging compared with framed construction, keeping interiors cooler in summer and warmer in winter. Our panel systems support high energy performance and can contribute to 8-Star NatHERS outcomes. We can supply R-value and thermal data for your specific panel thickness and application.

faqs.q: Are the panels fire and moisture resistant?
faqs.a: Our pre-finished panels use durable MgO (magnesium oxide) and fibre-cement facings that are moisture-stable and won't warp, rot or feed mould, and offer strong fire performance for residential and commercial use. For any project with specific fire or compliance requirements, we'll provide the relevant test data and specifications.

faqs.q: What can SIPs be used for?
faqs.a: A wide range of builds — new homes, granny flats and modular dwellings, extensions and additions, insulated roofing, patios and outdoor living, coldrooms, and commercial and industrial buildings. The same core benefits — speed, insulation, strength and long spans — apply across all of them.

faqs.q: Can the panels span long distances without extra support?
faqs.a: Yes. Our panels are engineered for long structural spans, which means fewer beams, posts and purlins, cleaner architectural lines and more usable open space. Maximum spans depend on panel type, thickness and load, so we'll engineer the layout to suit your design.

faqs.q: Do you supply Australia-wide, and is it Australian-made?
faqs.a: Yes. Quick Built Systems panels are Australian-made and engineered for Australian conditions. As a family-owned business with 25 years of modular building experience, we supply direct to builders, owner-builders and trades across the country. Talk to us about lead times and delivery to your site.

faqs.q: Do I need council approval to build with SIPs?
faqs.a: As with any structural building work, approval requirements depend on your project type, size and local council. SIP buildings are designed and certified to meet the National Construction Code (NCC), and we can provide engineering, specifications and compliance documentation to support your application. We recommend confirming requirements with your certifier or council early.

faqs.q: How do I get a quote?
faqs.a: Tell us about your project — what you're building, rough dimensions and your timeline — and our team will put together a no-obligation quote covering panel specs, supply options and pricing. Use the Get a free quote button, call us on 1300 132 787, or send your plans through the contact form to get started.
